entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum americanum (L.) Leeke, the crop studied for genotypic variation in ABA accumulation

entities.name: ABSCISIC ACID
entities.description: A plant hormone (ABA) that accumulates in response to water stress

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: Environmental condition characterized by lack of water, inducing ABA accumulation

entities.name: DETACHED LEAF TEST
entities.description: A laboratory test used to measure the capacity of genotypes to accumulate ABA

entities.name: BJ 104
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet with lower ABA accumulation capacity

entities.name: SERERE 39
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet with high ABA accumulation capacity

entities.name: B282
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet used in field experiments

entities.name: LEAF WATER POTENTIAL
entities.description: A measure of the water status of the leaf, denoted as ψ

entities.name: TURGOR POTENTIAL
entities.description: The pressure exerted by the cell contents against the cell wall, denoted as ψp

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where field experiments were conducted

entities.name: ABA ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet genotypes to accumulate abscisic acid in response to stress
